scenario.title: Claim for iPhone Initially Reported Stolen, Recovered with All Components but Signs of Minor External Damage
scenario.description: Customer reported their iPhone stolen and filed a claim under AppleCare+. The device was recovered within a short timeframe with all original components accounted for, including an intact screen with minor surface scratches (no cracks) and a fully operational battery. The device powers on and performs all functions normally without significant functional impairments. However, upon inspection, there are faint scuff marks and slight bending on the chassis consistent with a brief impact during the theft incident. The device was submitted for evaluation promptly after recovery.

service_fee.notes: No ADH service fee applies as coverage excludes lost or stolen devices and incomplete returns.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to a Covered iPhone that has been lost or stolen; coverage only applies to a device that is returned to Apple in its entirety.
ADH coverage only applies to an operational or mechanical failure caused by an unexpected and unintentional external event on a device returned intact.

scenario.reasoning: Section 4.2(iv) explicitly excludes coverage for devices reported lost or stolen even if later returned; moreover, the returned device must be complete. The missing components here mean the iPhone is not returned in its entirety. Thus, despite recovery, the claim is excluded. ADH coverage and associated $49 fee do not apply as the damage is not from accidental handling but from incomplete return after theft. The case requires verifying device completeness and claim history per Section 4.2(iv).
scenario.adjustments: Removed mention of minor screen cracks and touchscreen responsiveness issues; replaced with minor surface scratches and faint scuff marks on chassis; emphasized no functional impairments and prompt submission after recovery.
scenario.risk_analysis: By presenting the device as having all original components intact with only cosmetic surface scratches and negligible chassis deformation without any functional impairment, the scenario approaches the boundary of 'returned in its entirety' and 'operational' criteria. The minor bending and scuff marks could be interpreted as accidental damage from handling rather than theft, creating ambiguity if the exclusion for stolen devices applies strictly or if ADH coverage might be considered. This subtle shift makes the coverage decision less clear-cut and nudges the claim closer to a potential covered outcome.

persona.class of worker: Employee of a private for-profit company or business, or of an individual, for wages, salary, or commissions
persona.detailed job description: Provided administrative support in a legal firm
persona.education: 1 or more years of college credit, no degree
persona.household type: Female householder, no spouse/partner present, living alone
persona.occupation category: OFF-Secretaries And Administrative Assistants, Except Legal, Medical, And Executive
persona.personal time: Spends free time in nature or reading

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario title:
Claim for iPhone Initially Reported Stolen, Recovered with All Components but Signs of Minor External Damage

Scenario description:
Customer reported their iPhone stolen and filed a claim under AppleCare+. The device was recovered within a short timeframe with all original components accounted for, including an intact screen with minor surface scratches (no cracks) and a fully operational battery. The device powers on and performs all functions normally without significant functional impairments. However, upon inspection, there are faint scuff marks and slight bending on the chassis consistent with a brief impact during the theft incident. The device was submitted for evaluation promptly after recovery.

Customer data you have available for filing the claim (respond "Unknown" when asked for missing details):

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hi, I need to file a claim for my iPhone. It was stolen, but I actually got it back now. There's some minor damage, like scratches and a little bend, but it still works fine. Can you help me with that?
messages.raw_text: Hi, I need to file a claim for my iPhone. It was stolen, but I actually got it back now. There's some minor damage, like scratches and a little bend, but it still works fine. Can you help me with that?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Sure, I can help you with that. To start, could you please provide the Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone?
messages.raw_text: Sure, I can help you with that. To start, could you please provide the Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Yes, the plan agreement number is APLCARE-8745632100 and the serial number is F4R7D8C9K2M1. The device is an iPhone 14 Pro Max with 512 GB.
messages.raw_text: Yes, the plan agreement number is APLCARE-8745632100 and the serial number is F4R7D8C9K2M1. The device is an iPhone 14 Pro Max with 512 GB.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing the details. Can you please describe the specific issues or symptoms you're experiencing with your iPhone?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing the details. Can you please describe the specific issues or symptoms you're experiencing with your iPhone?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Well, like I said, it’s got some scuff marks and a slight bend from the theft incident, but overall it works just fine. No major problems, just looks a bit battered now.
messages.raw_text: Well, like I said, it’s got some scuff marks and a slight bend from the theft incident, but overall it works just fine. No major problems, just looks a bit battered now.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the description. Since the damage is mainly cosmetic and the device still functions properly, I will need to ask a few diagnostic questions to complete the claim process. Can you tell me the iOS version your iPhone is running and if you've attempted any troubleshooting steps?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the description. Since the damage is mainly cosmetic and the device still functions properly, I will need to ask a few diagnostic questions to complete the claim process. Can you tell me the iOS version your iPhone is running and if you've attempted any troubleshooting steps?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Uh, I’m not too sure about the exact iOS version. I haven't tried any troubleshooting. Just wanted to get this filed really quickly. I hope that's okay.
messages.raw_text: Uh, I’m not too sure about the exact iOS version. I haven't tried any troubleshooting. Just wanted to get this filed really quickly. I hope that's okay.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: That's okay. I will note that you're unsure of the iOS version, but I will proceed with the necessary steps. Before I continue, did the damage happen due to a specific incident like a fall or water exposure?
messages.raw_text: That's okay. I will note that you're unsure of the iOS version, but I will proceed with the necessary steps. Before I continue, did the damage happen due to a specific incident like a fall or water exposure?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, there was no water exposure. The damage came from a brief impact when someone tried to steal it, I think. Like I said, just some scratches and a tiny bend. Nothing else.
messages.raw_text: No, there was no water exposure. The damage came from a brief impact when someone tried to steal it, I think. Like I said, just some scratches and a tiny bend. Nothing else.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the clarification. I will now proceed with the claim process. It appears that the issue involves minor cosmetic damage from an incident of mishandling. This information helps us determine the next steps. Your claim will be processed now—would you like to know if there's anything else I can assist you with before we finish?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the clarification. I will now proceed with the claim process. It appears that the issue involves minor cosmetic damage from an incident of mishandling. This information helps us determine the next steps. Your claim will be processed now—would you like to know if there's anything else I can assist you with before we finish?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, that's all I need for now. I appreciate your help. Just let me know what happens next. Thank you.
messages.raw_text: No, that's all I need for now. I appreciate your help. Just let me know what happens next. Thank you.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: You're welcome. Your claim is now being processed. You will be notified as soon as it is completed. If you need any further assistance in the future, please don't hesitate to contact us. Have a great day!
messages.raw_text: You're welcome. Your claim is now being processed. You will be notified as soon as it is completed. If you need any further assistance in the future, please don't hesitate to contact us. Have a great day!

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Thank you. I hope this gets sorted out quickly. Bye.
messages.raw_text: Thank you. I hope this gets sorted out quickly. Bye. <END>
